authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.24 - Condition of participation: Medical record services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital medical-record regulation for Medicare and Medicaid participating hospitals; authoritative for EHR, encounter, ADT, and medical-record truth sets used in patient placement.
used_for: requiring EHR, encounter, and medical-record traceability
preventing throughput recommendations based only on informal status notes
anchoring auditability of patient-specific patient movement evidence

authoritative_sources.title: Admission, Discharge, and Transfer Patient Event Notification Conditions of Participation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS interprets the hospital ADT event notification Conditions of Participation and is authoritative for patient status event, notification, EHR, administrative system, and privacy-preference handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ADT status refresh, notification dependency review, and source hierarchy
used_for: treating ADT as a central hospital-patient-throughput status source
requiring status refresh before recommendations change
preserving privacy preferences and recipient restrictions in patient movement outputs

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.43 - Condition of participation: Discharge planning
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital discharge-planning regulation; authoritative for why capacity release and care-transition dependencies must be documented without hospital patient throughput deciding discharge readiness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Discharge-dependent capacity release and care-transition blocker review
used_for: separating capacity release forecasts from discharge-readiness decisions
requiring discharge and post-acute blockers to be captured as constraints
routing care-transition decisions to discharge planning or clinical owners

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.30 - Condition of participation: Utilization review
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital utilization-review regulation; authoritative for escalation when throughput decisions depend on admission status, medical necessity, continued stay, payer interpretation, or utilization review outcome.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Authorization, eligibility, admission-status, and utilization-review prerequisite handling
used_for: refusing to decide medical necessity or continued-stay status
routing payer or utilization interpretation to adjacent owners
blocking placement when tenant policy requires current UR or authorization evidence

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.23 - Condition of participation: Nursing services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital nursing-services regulation; authoritative for treating staffed-bed and care-team and licensed nursing constraints as real throughput availability constraints that require nursing or staffing-owner escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Staffed-throughput capacity and unit readiness assessment
used_for: distinguishing physical beds and treatment spaces from staffed, serviceable throughput capacity
escalating licensed staffing adequacy instead of approving closed-bed reopening
capturing staffing blockers in capacity shortfall summaries

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 489.24 - Special responsibilities of Medicare hospitals in emergency cases
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal EMTALA regulation; authoritative for emergency transfer capacity concepts involving beds, qualified staff, and equipment while keeping legal and clinical transfer acceptance conclusions outside this lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Transfer-center capacity fact packaging and legal or clinical escalation
used_for: packaging transfer capacity facts without acceptance or refusal authority
capturing qualified staff and equipment as constraints
escalating EMTALA-sensitive requests to legal, clinical, and transfer-center owners

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the federal HIPAA privacy regulator; authoritative for minimum-necessary PHI handling in census extracts, throughput-board notes, transfer packets, and escalation summaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Privacy and PHI handling gate review
used_for: limiting patient identifiers and diagnoses in outputs
refusing uncontrolled census or placement-list disclosure
routing privacy exceptions to healthcare privacy owners

authoritative_sources.title: Guideline for Isolation Precautions: Recommendations
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC is the federal public health authority for infection-control guidance; authoritative for patient-placement principles that make isolation, single-patient room, and cohorting questions safety-sensitive.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Isolation, cohorting, and infection-control blocker review
used_for: treating isolation uncertainty as a blocker
routing cohorting or infection-control clearance to qualified owners
preventing generic throughput allocation when infection risk changes placement

authoritative_sources.title: Improving Patient Flow and Reducing Emergency Department Crowding: A Guide for Hospitals
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ is a federal healthcare quality and patient-safety agency; this public hospital guide is authoritative for patient-flow, boarding, capacity, and hospitalwide throughput workflow framing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Demand intake, capacity assessment, sequencing, and refresh
used_for: ED boarding and hospitalwide flow scenario design
capacity shortfall, throughput, and escalation framing
distinguishing flow recommendations from final approval

authoritative_sources.title: Location - FHIR R4
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HL7 is a recognized healthcare interoperability standards body; FHIR Location is authoritative vendor-neutral terminology for facilities, wards, rooms, beds, treatment spaces, and operational location hierarchy.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Location, room, unit, bed, and treatment-space terminology normalization
used_for: normalizing hospital, unit, room, and bed hierarchy
separating physical location from assignment authority
supporting system-of-record retrieval expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Encounter - FHIR R4
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HL7 FHIR Encounter is authoritative vendor-neutral terminology for patient encounter status, class, hospitalization, participants, and location history used by EHR and ADT systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Encounter, location history, and ADT truth-set normalization
used_for: normalizing inpatient, observation, ED, transfer, and discharge states
requiring encounter context before placement recommendations
tracing location history and status changes

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule (CMS-0057-F)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS public policy for payer prior authorization APIs and decision transparency; authoritative for payer/provider prior authorization evidence dependencies without granting this lane payer interpretation authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Coverage, referral, authorization, and payer dependency handling
used_for: scenario coverage for missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependencies
routing payer-policy interpretation away from hospital patient throughput
preserving documented authorization status as a dependency rather than a throughput decision

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Throughput work requires tenant facility context, source hierarchy, ADT or encounter record, current throughput-board and unit capacity state, documented request source, accepting service or provider context, required order or transfer packet, staffed-bed, care-team, and unit-scope evidence, EVS or room readiness, isolation and equipment constraints, transport readiness, discharge or care-transition dependencies, and tenant priority or approval rules. Eligibility, referral, prior authorization, payer, and utilization review facts are prerequisites when tenant policy or the service setting makes them material, but payer interpretation remains adjacent.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: The truth set is established by EHR or EMR encounter and clinical documentation, ADT feed, hospital-patient-throughput board, capacity dashboard, unit census, staffed-bed and care-team roster, EVS and transport systems, transfer-center record, case-management and discharge planning notes, utilization review system, patient access or payer portal records, privacy or audit system, and tenant source-of-truth hierarchy.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops at recommendation-quality operational planning and evidence packaging. Licensed clinical authority begins at diagnosis, treatment planning, acuity, level-of-care, discharge readiness, infection-control clearance, and medical necessity. Privacy authority begins when PHI disclosure, consent, recipient, or minimum-necessary questions change the safe output. Payer or utilization authority begins when eligibility, referral, authorization, admission status, coverage, continued stay, coding, claim, denial, or appeal interpretation affects the answer.

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and placement-frame normalization
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the request is hospital patient-throughput work and normalize ED holds, direct admissions, interfacility transfers, post-op or PACU demand, ICU or stepdown moves, med-surg or telemetry placement, behavioral health placement, discharge pending, transport or diagnostic delays, closed beds, and room-clean states into a planning frame.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Truth-set and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate ADT, EHR encounter, throughput board, order or transfer request, accepting service, authorization or referral dependencies where material, privacy constraints, unit scope, and source-of-truth hierarchy before acting.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess staffed beds, treatment spaces, unit census, service-line fit, isolation and cohorting blockers, diagnostic or procedure delays, equipment readiness, EVS and transport readiness, nurse staffing, discharge dependencies, and downstream impacts.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Allocation and sequence recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare recommendation-only patient-flow sequencing, candidate units or treatment spaces, shortfall summaries, deferred-demand sets, blocked alternatives, discharge-impact notes, and escalation packages using approved priority rules and planning horizon.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Refresh, handoff, and exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: Refresh ADT, throughput board, EVS, transport, staffing, transfer-center, discharge, payer, and approval evidence; escalate when systems conflict, patient safety is implicated, or authority shifts to an adjacent owner.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Privacy, patient safety, and authority gate review
workflow_stages.description: Apply minimum-necessary PHI controls, refuse uncontrolled census disclosure, and route clinical, infection-control, utilization-review, transfer, legal, payer, privacy, staffing, or final approval decisions to accountable owners.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: Throughput request with source, service line, requested destination, priority flag, timing, and requesting owner
EHR or EMR encounter record, ADT status, location history, admission order, accepting service note, and clinical-administrative handoff notes
Throughput board, unit census, staffed-bed and care-team roster, physical bed and treatment-space inventory, capacity dashboard, closed-bed log, and surge status
EVS room-clean status, transport queue, equipment availability, isolation flag, cohorting note, and infection-prevention escalation record
Transfer-center packet, accepting provider record, discharge or care-transition note, case-management blocker, and post-acute placement status

Eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, utilization review, payer portal, inpatient or observation status, coding, charge capture, claim, denial, or appeal records where material to the throughput workflow
Tenant priority matrix, approval log, command-center decision record, exception log, constraint log, allocation recommendation, and escalation note
systems_of_record: EHR or EMR and associated clinical documentation systems
ADT feed or patient registration system
Throughput board, capacity dashboard, unit census, and physical bed and treatment-space inventory
Staffing or workforce management system and nursing supervisor roster
EVS or housekeeping room-turn system, transport system, and equipment tracking system
Transfer-center platform, case-management system, utilization review workflow, and discharge or care-transition tracker
Practice management, revenue cycle, payer portal, eligibility, referral, and prior authorization systems
Privacy, consent, audit, incident, command-center, and approval systems

allowed: Classify throughput work as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
Validate patient placement facts, throughput capacity, readiness, source records, and tenant policy inputs
Recommend throughput allocation, sequencing, deferral, shortfall, and escalation packages with explicit assumptions and authority limits
Prioritize work, capacity, or resources using documented constraints and approved tenant rules
Refresh throughput board, ADT, EVS, transport, staffing, transfer, discharge, payer, and approval evidence before changing recommendation state
escalate: Missing or conflicting ADT, EHR, throughput-board, staffed-bed and care-team, EVS, isolation, transport, transfer, discharge, authorization, or approval records
Clinical acuity, level-of-care, medical necessity, discharge readiness, diagnosis, treatment, infection-control, or patient-safety judgment is required
Payer policy, coverage, referral, authorization, admission status, continued stay, coding, claim, denial, or appeal interpretation changes the answer
PHI disclosure, privacy preference, consent, or minimum-necessary handling changes the safe output
Transfer acceptance or refusal is EMTALA-sensitive or requires legal, receiving-physician, transfer-center, or executive authority
Tenant, payer, public, facility, or jurisdictional rules conflict materially
refuse: Request to provide final throughput, staffing, transfer, surge, closed-bed reopening, discharge, or operational approval
Request to determine clinical urgency, level of care, medical necessity, treatment suitability, isolation clearance, or discharge readiness
Request to disclose named census, diagnosis, location, payer, or movement history outside role-based and minimum-necessary controls
Request to treat a generic room, facilities, or office scheduling prompt as hospital patient throughput without patient, encounter, ADT, or throughput-board evidence
Request to override missing prerequisites, source conflicts, payer dependencies, or tenant approval matrices

domain_failure_modes: The lane behaves like generic room scheduling and ignores ADT, encounter, service-line, staffed-bed and care-team, isolation, discharge, and patient-safety evidence.
A throughput slot is recommended as ready even though EHR, ADT, throughput board, EVS, transport, staffing, isolation, or equipment systems materially conflict.

Physical beds, treatment spaces, or discharge pathways are counted as available when nursing staffing, unit scope, transport, diagnostics, or downstream care-transition blockers make them unavailable for safe flow.
The lane implies clinical acuity, level-of-care, discharge readiness, infection-control, or medical-necessity authority.
A planned admission, transfer, or post-acute dependency proceeds despite missing eligibility, referral, authorization, payer, or utilization review evidence required by tenant policy.
Census, isolation, diagnosis, payer, or movement-history PHI is exported or shared without minimum-necessary controls.
A transfer-center scenario is treated as simple throughput availability and omits qualified staff, equipment, receiving-physician, legal, or EMTALA-sensitive escalation.
Public, payer, and tenant policies conflict but the lane synthesizes a confident answer instead of escalating.
Tenant source-of-truth hierarchy, unit taxonomy, priority matrix, surge rules, and approval authorities are guessed rather than retrieved.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant
Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific throughput priority matrix and surge command authority
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution: The public baseline cannot define a universal priority order or surge approval owner. The package records these as required tenant facts and refuses deterministic prioritization when missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Payer, plan, referral, authorization, and utilization review differences
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution: The package treats payer and UR facts as prerequisites when material but routes interpretation and approval to adjacent owners.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific transfer, privacy, and facility policy variation
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution: The package requires jurisdiction and tenant policy retrieval, escalates conflicts, and does not claim legal, privacy, or EMTALA compliance authority.
